    31‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.

The docstring also confirms it was *"resolved in the affirmative"*, so the problem is solved with answer "yes". Per Section 6 Case 1, the theorem should be:

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/290.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 290`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/290`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Bibliography entries present for `[ErGr80]` and `[vD24]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos290 ... end Erdos290`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos290`).
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `harmonicPartialSum` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Not marked `noncomputable` ‚Äî correct since `‚Ñö` arithmetic over a decidable `Finset` is computable.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `harmonicPartialSum` uses `lowerCamelCase` (function returning a value).
- [ ] *(Optional)* No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity check for `harmonicPartialSum`. Not required but could be useful for verifying the definition (e.g., confirming the example in the docstring that `harmonicPartialSum 3 5 = 47/60`).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_290` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] Has a docstring referencing source `[ErGr80, p.34]`.

### `answer()` usage

- [ ] **FAIL ‚Äî Missing `answer()` wrapper (Case 1 applies).** The module docstring states: *"Must there exist some $b > a$ such that‚Ä¶"* ‚Äî this is a yes/no question ("Must there exist" maps to "does there exist" per the checklist). The docstring also confirms it was *"resolved in the affirmative"*, so the problem is solved with answer "yes". Per Section 6 Case 1, the theorem should be:

  ```lean
  theorem erdos_290 : answer(True) ‚Üî
      ‚àÄ a : ‚Ñï, 1 ‚â§ a ‚Üí
      ‚àÉ b : ‚Ñï, a < b ‚àß
        (harmonicPartialSum a (b + 1)).den < (harmonicPartialSum a b).den := by
    sorry
  ```

  Currently the theorem uses a direct assertion (Case 3 form) without `answer()`. Since the English phrasing is a question, Case 1 is required.

- [x] Quantifiers (`‚àÄ a`) are in the theorem body, not as parameters ‚Äî correct placement for `answer()` usage (once added).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[category research solved]`.
- [x] `research solved` is a valid category value (solved but not formally verified ‚Äî `sorry` in proof is expected).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Has exactly one `@[AMS 11]` attribute on the same line as `category`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] Only one number ‚Äî ordering and duplicate checks trivially pass.
- [x] `11` (Number theory) is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` format (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the solved problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚Äî no variants present.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`‚Ñï`, `‚Ñö`, `‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚àß`, `‚â§`, `<`, `‚Üë`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all well under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified ‚Äî `lake build` not run during this review.

---

## Summary

**1 blocking issue:**

1. **Missing `answer(True) ‚Üî ...` wrapper.** The problem is phrased as a yes/no question ("Must there exist‚Ä¶") and was resolved affirmatively. Per the checklist ¬ß6 Case 1, the theorem must use `answer(True) ‚Üî P`, not a bare assertion. This would also be caught by `linter.style.answer_attribute` if the linter expects `answer()` for questions.

**Everything else passes.** The copyright header, imports, docstring, namespace, definition style, category/AMS attributes, proof body format, and code quality all conform to the checklist.

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 290

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/290](https://www.erdosproblems.com/290)

Let $a \ge 1$. Must there exist some $b > a$ such that
$$\sum_{a \le n \le b} 1/n = r_1/s_1 \quad \text{and} \quad \sum_{a \le n \le b+1} 1/n = r_2/s_2,$$
with $(r_i, s_i) = 1$ and $s_2 < s_1$?

For example, $\sum_{3 \le n \le 5} 1/n = 47/60$ and $\sum_{3 \le n \le 6} 1/n = 19/20$,
and $20 < 60$.

This was resolved in the affirmative by van Doorn [vD24], who proved $b = b(a)$
always exists and $b(a) \ll a$. More precisely, $b(a) < 4.374a$ for all $a > 1$,
and $b(a) > a + 0.54 \log a$ for all large $a$.

[ErGr80] Erd≈ës, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
theory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

[vD24] van Doorn, F., *On the denominators of harmonic numbers* (2024).
-/

open Finset

namespace Erdos290

/-- The partial harmonic sum $\sum_{n=a}^{b} 1/n$ as a rational number. -/
def harmonicPartialSum (a b : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñö :=
  (Finset.Icc a b).sum (fun n => (1 : ‚Ñö) / ‚Üën)

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 290 (Proved) [ErGr80, p.34]:

For every $a \ge 1$, there exists $b > a$ such that the denominator of
$\sum_{n=a}^{b+1} 1/n$ (in lowest terms) is strictly less than the denominator
of $\sum_{n=a}^{b} 1/n$ (in lowest terms).
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_290 :
    ‚àÄ a : ‚Ñï, 1 ‚â§ a ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ b : ‚Ñï, a < b ‚àß
      (harmonicPartialSum a (b + 1)).den < (harmonicPartialSum a b).den := by
  sorry

end Erdos290

You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

See the License for the specific language governing permissions and

-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 290

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/290](https://www.erdosproblems.com/290)

Let $a \ge 1$. Must there exist some $b > a$ such that
$$\sum_{a \le n \le b} 1/n = r_1/s_1 \quad \text{and} \quad \sum_{a \le n \le b+1} 1/n = r_2/s_2,$$
with $(r_i, s_i) = 1$ and $s_2 < s_1$?

For example, $\sum_{3 \le n \le 5} 1/n = 47/60$ and $\sum_{3 \le n \le 6} 1/n = 19/20$,
and $20 < 60$.

This was resolved in the affirmative by van Doorn [vD24], who proved $b = b(a)$
always exists and $b(a) \ll a$. More precisely, $b(a) < 4.374a$ for all $a > 1$,
and $b(a) > a + 0.54 \log a$ for all large $a$.

[ErGr80] Erd≈ës, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
theory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

[vD24] van Doorn, F., *On the denominators of harmonic numbers* (2024).
-/

open Finset

namespace Erdos290

/-- The partial harmonic sum $\sum_{n=a}^{b} 1/n$ as a rational number. -/
def harmonicPartialSum (a b : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñö :=
  (Finset.Icc a b).sum (fun n => (1 : ‚Ñö) / ‚Üën)

/--
Erd≈ës Problem 290 (Proved) [ErGr80, p.34]:

For every $a \ge 1$, there exists $b > a$ such that the denominator of
$\sum_{n=a}^{b+1} 1/n$ (in lowest terms) is strictly less than the denominator
of $\sum_{n=a}^{b} 1/n$ (in lowest terms).
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_290 :
    ‚àÄ a : ‚Ñï, 1 ‚â§ a ‚Üí
    ‚àÉ b : ‚Ñï, a < b ‚àß
      (harmonicPartialSum a (b + 1)).den < (harmonicPartialSum a b).den := by
  sorry

end Erdos290

content.text: Done. Added the `answer(True) ‚Üî` wrapper to `erdos_290` since the problem is a yes/no question ("Must there exist‚Ä¶") resolved affirmatively, which per the checklist requires Case 1 form.
